description:
Goal: move the semantic part of native codegen into compiler2 ownership. Signal: compiler2 lowers `NativeProgram(root)` through compiler2-owned codegen modules that consume compiler2 `Ty`, compiler2 dispatch/type-test facts, callable-entry inventory, extern marshal facts, and body ownership directly; only truly universal CLIF/backend/runtime mechanics remain shared.

Change:
- copy/adapt the native-codegen modules whose behavior depends on compiler2 facts out of `src/ir_codegen` into compiler2
- retarget those modules onto compiler2-owned surfaces instead of planner-shaped wrappers and old-world associated-type pins
- leave shared only the substrate that turns already-settled ABI/layout/runtime facts into machine code and linked artifacts

Why:
- compiler2 cannot be free of old dead weight while the logic that interprets its facts lives inside a backend path shaped around the old compiler

Checks:
- compiler2-native tests enter the compiler2-owned backend directly
- no compiler2-owned lowering module depends on `SpecPlan`, `SpecRegistry`, or `crate::types::Ty`
title: Compiler2 native backends: bring semantic native codegen in-house
